risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to high heat dissipation from the harmonic drive.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management solutions such as cooling channels and heat sinks.

risk.desc: Risk of cable strain due to inadequate strain relief geometry.
risk.mit: Ensure all cable exits have strain relief features and proper bend radius.

risk.desc: Risk of misalignment during assembly due to unclear assembly sequence.
risk.mit: Define a clear and detailed assembly sequence with tool and torque specifications.

risk.desc: Risk of structural failure due to insufficient reinforcement in critical areas.
risk.mit: Add structural reinforcement ribs in areas subjected to high loads.

risk.desc: Risk of improper fit due to lack of tolerance stack-up information.
risk.mit: Include tolerance stack-up information for all interfaces.

verify.rule: All required fields and structures are present.
Mass estimate is a numeric value and within expected range.
P0 internal_feature_targets appear as part_tree children and geometry_ops ids or targets.
Each interface has a named target part, connection type, DOF, clearance, and required feature.
Critical joints have visible insert/washer/locking/preload evidence and torque or preload intent.
Boundary conditions are defined with fixture points, thermal zones, and pressure faces.

risk.desc: Potential for cable interference with moving parts during full range of motion.
risk.mit: Implement slack loops and strain relief at each joint.

risk.desc: Risk of improper assembly sequence leading to inaccessible fasteners.
risk.mit: Define clear assembly and disassembly sequences in cad_brief.

risk.desc: Risk of insufficient structural integrity due to consolidation of parts.
risk.mit: Ensure load paths are closed and high-load joints use appropriate connection types.

risk.desc: Potential for thermal expansion issues affecting cable routing.
risk.mit: Include thermal expansion allowances in harness routing design.

risk.desc: Risk of inadequate service access for cable management.
risk.mit: Design service access panels and ensure harness visibility.

physics_paths.path_description: Drag chain or spiral cable carrier from base to wrist, providing signal, power, and pneumatic connections.

integration_opportunities.description: Harnesses, tubes, or service channels are routed through visible raceways or protected channels.
Every cable exit shows strain relief geometry (clamp, grommet, tie point) and routing respects a stated minimum bend radius away from moving joints and sharp edges.
Harness crossing each joint has slack loop, strain relief at both ends, and routing that avoids pinch points through the full motion range.

verification_focus.description: Parts can be installed in a stated order where every fastener, connector, and mating feature stays reachable; no installed part blocks access to a later one; last-installed and captive parts are identified.
Every part's primary load traces through named interfaces to the base/ground; the load path closes for all parts and high-load joints use load-appropriate connection types.

Compared to the previous version the design moves the right direction: fewer interfaces, parts consolidated where service is preserved, open load paths closed, and redundancy added only at critical joints.
